positions.caption: Starting position
positions.text: Count every checker: multiply its point number by the number of checkers on it, then add everything up. \nBoth players start at exactly 167.

positions.caption: A typical bear off position
positions.text: Pipcount Player: 51 pips - Opponent: 60 pips

positions.caption: Typical Holding game
positions.text:
Players checkers: We know there are 42 pips on the closed board. Two checkers are missing on the 5th, so 32 pips. 1 checker on the 10th. That makes 42. 2 checkers on the 21st = 42. That makes 84. 1 checker on the 16th, that makes 100, + 1 checker on the 13th = total 113

There are certainly several ways to count the opponent’s checkers. For example, we mentally move the checker from 8 and 6 to 7, giving 11 × 7 = 77. 
2 checker on 5 = 87. 
2 checker on 2 = 91.

With a little practice, counting a position like this takes less than 5 seconds.

positions.caption: Reference Position 1:  5-Prime
positions.text:
Multiply the midpoint of any 5-prime by 10 and you have just counted a cluster of ten checkers.
This position shows a 5-prime from the 4-point to the 8-point.
The 6-point is the midpoint and the count for these ten checkers = 60 pips (6 × 10.) This is so because 5's and 7's average out to 6's, and 4's and 8's also average out to 6's.

PipCount = 60.

positions.caption: Reference Position 2:  Closed Board
positions.text:
This is just a 5-prime around the 4-point plus two checkers on the ace point.

PipCount = 42.

positions.caption: Reference Position 3
positions.text:
Five checkers each on the 6- and 8-points.

PipCount = 70.

positions.caption: Reference Position 4
positions.text:
Two checkers each on the 7- and 8-points.

PipCount = 30.

positions.caption: Reference Position 5
positions.text:
Five checkers on the 8-point.

PipCount = 40.

positions.caption: Reference Position 6
positions.text:
Two checkers each on the midpoint and opponent's bar point.

PipCount = 62.

positions.caption: Reference Position 7
positions.text:
Two checkers on the midpoint and one on the 14 point.

PipCount = 40.

positions.caption: Using 5-Point as Key Point
positions.text:
This position shows two examples of counting a cluster of eight checkers all at once as if they were eight 5's = 40.

Player = 40. Opponent = 40.

positions.caption: Using 20-Point as Key Point
positions.text:
The 20-point (opponent's 5-point) is the most useful key point. All checkers in your opponent's home board should be counted as 20 plus the pips required to get to the 20-point.

Player's count is 108 which can be visualized as five 20's + 4 (two each from the 22-point to the 20-point) + 4 (one from 24-point to 20 point).

Opponent's count is 89, visualized as four 20's + 4 + 5 (for the checker on the bar).

positions.caption: Mirror - Example 1
positions.text:
Player: (20 + 5 = 25) × 2 = 50.

Opponent: (13 + 12 = 25) × 2 = 50.

Player = 50. Opponent = 50.

positions.caption: One-Way Mental Shift
positions.text:
Player's checker can be easily counted in three clusters: 40 (eight 5's) + 33 (RP4 + 3 pips) + 64 (three 20's + 4) = 137.

Divide Opponent's checkers into three clusters: 44 (5-prime + 4 pips forward, 2 each from the 7-point to the 5-point) + 33 (three 10's + 3 pips from 13 to 10) + 44 (two 20's + 4) = 121.

Player = 137. Opponent = 121.

Pipcount Player: Opponent:

One way to count: Player: mental shifting 2 checkers 7 -> 6 and 2 checkers 5 -> 6 = 7x6 ‎ = 42
+ 1 checker on 8 = 50
+ 1 checker on 11 = 61
+ checkers on 2 and 3 = 10  ; 61 +10 ‎ = 71
+ 45 (checkers on 23 and 22) 71+45 = 116

Opponent:
5 prime over 6 = 10x6 ‎ = 60
+ one checker on 8 = 68
+ 3 checkers on 11 = 33 -> 68 + 33 ‎ = 101
+1 checker on 24 = 101+24 = 125

positions.caption: Two-Way Mental Shift
positions.text:
Player's spare checkers on the 6- and 8- points are on the same side of the board. By shifting them one pip in opposite directions to the 7-point, a 5-prime is formed. Player's position can easily be counted in two clusters: 70 (5-prime) + 65 (five 13's) = 135.

Opponent's spare checkers on the 8- and 13-points are on opposite sides of the board. By shifting them in the same direction, in this case left-to-right, a 5-prime is formed (RP1) and RP7 is also formed. Opponent's position can then be counted in three clusters: 60 + 40 + 42 (two 20's + 2) = 142.

Player = 135. Opponent = 142.

One way to count: Player: mental shifting one checker from 6 to 5, one checker from 4 to 5 ; one checker from 8 to 3, we get a five prime with center over 4. We know this is 10x4 ‎ = 40 . Don’t forget to add 5 from shifting 8 to 3 = 45 2 checkers on 8 (one checker ist shifted) = 16 ; 45 + 16 = 61
3 checker on 13 = 39 ; 61 + 39‎  =  100

Opponent: shift one checker for 6 to 4 and one checker from 2 to 4 = closed board. We know closed board = 42 + one checker on 7 = 49
+ 2 checker on 18 = 36 + 49 = 85

One way to count: Player: mental shifting one checker from 1 to 2, we get 3 checker on 2, 3 and 4. This is the same as 9 checker on 3 = 3 x 9 = 27
Don’t forget to subtract  1 from shifting 1 to 2 = 26
2 checkers on 6 -> 26 + 12  = 38 2 checkers on 8  = 16 ; 38 + 16 = 54
2 checker on 13 = 26 ; 54 + 26 ‎ =  80

Opponent: shift one checker for 6 to 4 and one checker from 2 to 4 = closed board. We know closed board = 42 + one checker on 7 = 49
+ 2 checker on 18 = 36 + 49 = 85

Player:
9 x 2 = 18

Opponent:

6 + 5 + 8 (2x4) + 4 (2x2) = 23

Player:
Shift two checkers from 9 to 8 —> 5 prime over 6 = 10 x 6 = 60 
Don’t forget to add 2 from shifting 9 to 8 = 62
2 checkers on 7 -> 62 + 14 = 76 
3 checkers on 6 -> 76 + 18 = 94 

Opponent:
Shift one checker from 3 to 2 —> 5 prime over 4 = 10 x 4 = 40 
Don’t forget to add 1 from shifting 3 to 2 = 41
Add 2 checker form 6 = 53
One checker on 11, 10 an 8 = 53 + 11 + 10 + 8 = 82

Player:
Shift one checker from 4 to 3 —> 5 prime over 5 = 10 x 5 = 50 
Don’t forget to add 1 from shifting 4 to 3 = 51
2 checkers on 7 -> 51 + 14 = 65 
3 checkers on 6 -> 65 + 18 = 83 

Opponent:
Shift one checker from 3 to 2 —> 5 prime over 4 = 10 x 4 = 40 
Don’t forget to add 1 from shifting 3 to 2 = 41
Add 2 checker form 6 = 53
One checker on 11, 10 an 8 = 53 + 11 + 10 + 8 = 82

Player:
Shift one checker from 4 to 3 —> 5 prime over 5 = 10 x 5 = 50 
Don’t forget to add 1 from shifting 4 to 3 = 51
2 checkers on 7 -> 51 + 14 = 65 
3 checkers on 6 -> 65 + 18 = 83 

Opponent:
Shift one checker from 3 to 2 —> 5 prime over 4 = 10 x 4 = 40 
Don’t forget to add 1 from shifting 3 to 2 = 41
Add one checker form 5 = 46
3 checker on 6 = 46 + 18 = 64
One checker on 10 = 64 + 10 = 74
